🐴 Klip Klop Maker — Scene Verification Report

Every bundled scene laid out, simulated with the rimless-wheel + sliding dynamics engine (js/simulate.js — the same code the Jest harness runs), and checked against its embedded expectations. Charts: speed trace with regime underlays (walk slide) and the descent profile.

verdictsceneoutcomeexpectedtimeclackspeak mm/s

✅ First Ramp 01-first-ramp

Smallest working track: two straight ramps at 11° with the washboard floor. The calibration baseline — if a printed figure doesn't trot here, tune the figure before building towers.

expectationactual
✔outcome = arrivedarrived
✔time ≤ 12s2.438s
✔clacks ≥ 1519
✔walked ≥ 95%100%
✔layout errors = [none][none]
✔energy budget (no energy creation)holds

✅ Slippery Slide (failure demo) 05-slippery-slide

16° on a smooth PLA floor: tan(16°) exceeds the grip budget, so the hooves never catch — the figure skis the whole track like a toboggan and brakes to a stop in the corral. Demonstrates the SLIDE regime and why the washboard floor exists.

expectationactual
✔outcome = arrivedarrived
✔time ≤ 20s2.142s

✔peak speed ≥ 300 mm/s557 mm/s

✔energy budget (no energy creation)holds
outcome arrived · 2.142s · 0 clacks · peak 557 mm/s · walked 0% · drop 155 mm

✅ Too Shallow (stall demo) 06-too-shallow-stall

8° slope, smooth floor, and a gritty axle (η=0.15): the energy recovered per step can't vault the stance leg over top dead center. The figure never gets going — the STALL regime.

expectationactual
✔outcome = stalledstalled
✔time ≤ 10s0s
✔walked ≤ 10%0%
✔layout errors = [none][none]

✅ Cliffhanger (tumble demo) 07-cliffhanger-tumble

A 14° slope against a tight ±12° swing limiter: the next hoof lands beyond the limiter's reach and the figure pitches over its front axle at the first ramp. The TUMBLE regime — why the swing stops and slope lock exist together.

expectationactual
✔outcome = tumbledtumbled
✔time ≤ 5s0s
✔layout errors = [none][none]
✔energy budget (no energy creation)holds

✅ Tight Radius (layout-violation demo) 08-tight-radius-jam

A 100 mm curve radius, below the 120 mm rigid-body minimum. The point-mass dynamics happily complete the run, but the layout validator flags that a real rectangular figure would wedge front-inner/rear-outer against the walls — a geometry failure the physics trace alone cannot see.

expectationactual
✔outcome = arrivedarrived
✔time ≤ 30s4.832s
✔layout errors = [radius-too-tight][radius-too-tight]
✔energy budget (no energy creation)holds
outcome arrived · 4.832s · 39 clacks · peak 131 mm/s · walked 100% · drop 121 mm

✅ Switchyard 09-switchyard

A gated Y-junction: the switch's vane routes the horse either straight ahead to corral A or through the diverging curve to corral B. The gate is set to the branch route — flip it in the app and re-run to watch the other ending.

✅ Lift & Return 10-lift-and-return

Three powered lift sections haul the figure ~90 mm uphill (externally driven conveyor — the one exception to gravity), then a 180° descent brings it trotting home. Demonstrates the LIFT regime and the energy-budget reset at powered spans.

expectationactual
✔outcome = arrivedarrived
✔time ≤ 35s14.326s
✔clacks ≥ 3048

✅ Palm Resort 11-palm-resort

S-curves through a decorated island resort: palm islands, a patio parking pad for figures, and a stacked tower — all on the standard interlocking hex-socket base system. A right-hand switch offers a shortcut to the beach corral.

✅ Perpetual Motion Ring 12-perpetual-motion

A closed circuit: three powered lifts climb each straightaway and four descending curves spend the height — the ring closes back on its own first piece with a legal 1.7 mm waterfall step. The figure trots laps forever (verified over 3 laps).

✅ Grand Circuit 13-grand-circuit

A stadium loop with longer straightaways: four lifts and a trot section per side, U-turn descents at each end. Demonstrates mixing walk and lift regimes inside one closed circuit.
